verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem plenus Spiritu Sancto regressus est a Jordane: et agebatur a Spiritu in desertum
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς δὲ πνεύματος ἁγίου πλήρης ὑπέστρεψεν ἀπὸ τοῦ ἰορδάνου καὶ ἤγετο ἐν τῷ πνεύματι εἰς τήν ἔρημον
verses.king_james: And Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost returned from Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,

verses.latin_vulgate: diebus quadraginta, et tentabatur a diabolo. Et nihil manducavit in diebus illis: et consummatis illis esuriit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμέρας τεσσαράκοντα πειραζόμενος ὑπὸ τοῦ διαβόλου καὶ οὐκ ἔφαγεν οὐδὲν ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἐκείναις καὶ συντελεσθεισῶν αὐτῶν ὕστερον ἐπείνασεν
verses.king_james: Being forty days tempted of the devil. And in those days he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem illi diabolus: Si Filius Dei es, dic lapidi huic ut panis fiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ διάβολος εἰ υἱὸς εἶ τοῦ θεοῦ εἰπὲ τῷ λίθῳ τούτῳ ἵνα γένηται ἄρτος
verses.king_james: And the devil said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondit ad illum Jesus: Scriptum est: Quia non in solo pane vivit homo, sed in omni verbo Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπεκρίθη ἰησοῦς πρὸς αὐτὸν λέγων, γέγραπται ὅτι οὐκ ἐπ᾽ ἄρτῳ μόνῳ ζήσεται ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἀλλ᾽ ἐπὶ παντὶ ῥήματι θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et duxit illum diabolus in montem excelsum, et ostendit illi omnia regna orbis terræ in momento temporis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναγαγὼν αὐτὸν ὁ διάβολος εἰς ὄρος ὑψηλὸν ἔδειξεν αὐτῷ πάσας τὰς βασιλείας τῆς οἰκουμένης ἐν στιγμῇ χρόνου
verses.king_james: And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait illi: Tibi dabo potestatem hanc universam, et gloriam illorum: quia mihi tradita sunt, et cui volo do illa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ διάβολος σοὶ δώσω τὴν ἐξουσίαν ταύτην ἅπασαν καὶ τὴν δόξαν αὐτῶν ὅτι ἐμοὶ παραδέδοται καὶ ᾧ ἐὰν θέλω δίδωμι αὐτήν
verses.king_james: And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu ergo si adoraveris coram me, erunt tua omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ οὖν ἐὰν προσκυνήσῃς ἐνώπιον μοῦ, ἔσται σοῦ πάντα
verses.king_james: If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Jesus, dixit illi: Scriptum est: Dominum Deum tuum adorabis, et illi soli servies.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς αὐτῷ εἶπεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ὑπαγε ὀπίσω μου, σατανᾶ γέγραπται γὰρ προσκυνήσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου καὶ αὐτῷ μόνῳ λατρεύσεις
verses.king_james: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et duxit illum in Jerusalem, et statuit eum super pinnam templi, et dixit illi: Si Filius Dei es, mitte te hinc deorsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤγαγεν αὐτὸν εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ ἔστησεν αὐτὸν ἐπὶ τὸ πτερύγιον τοῦ ἱεροῦ καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ εἰ ὁ υἱὸς εἶ τοῦ θεοῦ βάλε σεαυτὸν ἐντεῦθεν κάτω
verses.king_james: And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:

verses.latin_vulgate: Scriptum est enim quod angelis suis mandavit de te, ut conservent te:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γέγραπται γὰρ ὅτι τοῖς ἀγγέλοις αὐτοῦ ἐντελεῖται περὶ σοῦ τοῦ διαφυλάξαι σε
verses.king_james: For it is written, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: et quia in manibus tollent te, ne forte offendas ad lapidem pedem tuum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτι ἐπὶ χειρῶν ἀροῦσίν σε μήποτε προσκόψῃς πρὸς λίθον τὸν πόδα σου
verses.king_james: And in *their* hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Jesus, ait illi: Dictum est: Non tentabis Dominum Deum tuum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ὅτι εἴρηται οὐκ ἐκπειράσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering said unto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et consummata omni tentatione, diabolus recessit ab illo, usque ad tempus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συντελέσας πάντα πειρασμὸν ὁ διάβολος ἀπέστη ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ ἄχρι καιροῦ
verses.king_james: And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et regressus est Jesus in virtute Spiritus in Galilæam, et fama exiit per universam regionem de illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑπέστρεψεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐν τῇ δυνάμει τοῦ πνεύματος εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν καὶ φήμη ἐξῆλθεν καθ᾽ ὅλης τῆς περιχώρου περὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipse docebat in synagogis eorum, et magnificabatur ab omnibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς ἐδίδασκεν ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς αὐτῶν δοξαζόμενος ὑπὸ πάντων
verses.king_james: And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit Nazareth, ubi erat nutritus, et intravit secundum consuetudinem suam die sabbati in synagogam, et surrexit legere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν ναζαρέτ οὗ ἦν τεθραμμένος καὶ εἰσῆλθεν κατὰ τὸ εἰωθὸς αὐτῷ ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῶν σαββάτων εἰς τὴν συναγωγήν καὶ ἀνέστη ἀναγνῶναι
verses.king_james: And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et traditus est illi liber Isaiæ prophetæ. Et ut revolvit librum, invenit locum ubi scriptum erat:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπεδόθη αὐτῷ βιβλίον ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου καὶ ἀναπτύξας τὸ βιβλίον εὗρεν τὸν τόπον οὗ ἦν γεγραμμένον
verses.king_james: And there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet Esaias. And when he had opened the book, he found the place where it was written,

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus Domini super me: propter quod unxit me, evangelizare pauperibus misit me, sanare contritos corde, prædicare captivis remissionem, et cæcis visum, dimittere confractos in remissionem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πνεῦμα κυρίου ἐπ᾽ ἐμέ οὗ ἕνεκεν ἔχρισέν με εὐαγγελίζεσθαι πτωχοῖς ἀπέσταλκέν με ἰὰσασθαι τοὺς συντετριμμένους τὴν καρδίαν, κηρύξαι αἰχμαλώτοις ἄφεσιν καὶ τυφλοῖς ἀνάβλεψιν ἀποστεῖλαι τεθραυσμένους ἐν ἀφέσει
verses.king_james: The Spirit of the Lord *is* upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,

verses.latin_vulgate: prædicare annum Domini acceptum et diem retributionis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κηρύξαι ἐνιαυτὸν κυρίου δεκτόν
verses.king_james: To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum plicuisset librum, reddit ministro, et sedit. Et omnium in synagoga oculi erant intendentes in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πτύξας τὸ βιβλίον ἀποδοὺς τῷ ὑπηρέτῃ ἐκάθισεν καὶ πάντων ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ ἦσαν ἀτενίζοντες αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he closed the book, and he gave *it* again to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cœpit autem dicere ad illos: Quia hodie impleta est hæc scriptura in auribus vestris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤρξατο δὲ λέγειν πρὸς αὐτοὺς ὅτι σήμερον πεπλήρωται ἡ γραφὴ αὕτη ἐν τοῖς ὠσὶν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And he began to say unto them, This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnes testimonium illi dabant: et mirabantur in verbis gratiæ, quæ procedebant de ore ipsius, et dicebant: Nonne hic est filius Joseph?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάντες ἐμαρτύρουν αὐτῷ καὶ ἐθαύμαζον ἐπὶ τοῖς λόγοις τῆς χάριτος τοῖς ἐκπορευομένοις ἐκ τοῦ στόματος αὐτοῦ καὶ ἔλεγον οὐχ οὗτος ἐστιν ὁ υἱός ἰωσὴφ
verses.king_james: And all bare him witness, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Joseph's son?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Utique dicetis mihi hanc similitudinem: Medice cura teipsum: quanta audivimus facta in Capharnaum, fac et hic in patria tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς πάντως ἐρεῖτέ μοι τὴν παραβολὴν ταύτην ἰατρέ θεράπευσον σεαυτόν ὅσα ἠκούσαμεν γενόμενα ἐν τῇ καπερναούμ, ποίησον καὶ ὧδε ἐν τῇ πατρίδι σου
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem: Amen dico vobis, quia nemo propheta acceptus est in patria sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δέ ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐδεὶς προφήτης δεκτός ἐστιν ἐν τῇ πατρίδι αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.

verses.latin_vulgate: In veritate dico vobis, multæ viduæ erant in diebus Eliæ in Israël, quando clausum est cælum annis tribus et mensibus sex, cum facta esset fames magna in omni terra:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπ᾽ ἀληθείας δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν πολλαὶ χῆραι ἦσαν ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἠλίου ἐν τῷ ἰσραήλ ὅτε ἐκλείσθη ὁ οὐρανὸς ἐπὶ ἔτη τρία καὶ μῆνας ἕξ ὡς ἐγένετο λιμὸς μέγας ἐπὶ πᾶσαν τὴν γῆν
verses.king_james: But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;

verses.latin_vulgate: et ad nullam illarum missus est Elias, nisi in Sarepta Sidoniæ, ad mulierem viduam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πρὸς οὐδεμίαν αὐτῶν ἐπέμφθη ἠλίας εἰ μὴ εἰς σάρεπτα τῆς σιδῶνος πρὸς γυναῖκα χήραν
verses.king_james: But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, *a city* of Sidon, unto a woman *that was* a widow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et multi leprosi erant in Israël sub Eliseo propheta: et nemo eorum mundatus est nisi Naaman Syrus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πολλοὶ λεπροὶ ἦσαν ἐπὶ ἐλισσαίου τοῦ προφήτου ἐν τῷ ἰσραὴλ καὶ οὐδεὶς αὐτῶν ἐκαθαρίσθη εἰ μὴ νεεμὰν ὁ σύρος
verses.king_james: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et repleti sunt omnes in synagoga ira, hæc audientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπλήσθησαν πάντες θυμοῦ ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ ἀκούοντες ταῦτα
verses.king_james: And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surrexerunt, et ejecerunt illum extra civitatem: et duxerunt illum usque ad supercilium montis, super quem civitas illorum erat ædificata, ut præcipitarent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναστάντες ἐξέβαλον αὐτὸν ἔξω τῆς πόλεως καὶ ἤγαγον αὐτὸν ἕως τῆς ὀφρύος τοῦ ὄρους ἐφ᾽ οὗ ἡ πόλις αὐτῶν ᾠκοδόμητο εἰς τὸ κατακρημνίσαι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse autem transiens per medium illorum, ibat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτὸς δὲ διελθὼν διὰ μέσου αὐτῶν ἐπορεύετο
verses.king_james: But he passing through the midst of them went his way,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et descendit in Capharnaum civitatem Galilææ, ibique docebat illos sabbatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατῆλθεν εἰς καπερναοὺμ πόλιν τῆς γαλιλαίας καὶ ἦν διδάσκων αὐτοὺς ἐν τοῖς σάββασιν
verses.king_james: And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et stupebant in doctrina ejus, quia in potestate erat sermo ipsius.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξεπλήσσοντο ἐπὶ τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ ὅτι ἐν ἐξουσίᾳ ἦν ὁ λόγος αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in synagoga erat homo habens dæmonium immundum, et exclamavit voce magna,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ ἦν ἄνθρωπος ἔχων πνεῦμα δαιμονίου ἀκαθάρτου καὶ ἀνέκραξεν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ
verses.king_james: And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil, and cried out with a loud voice,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Sine, quid nobis et tibi, Jesu Nazarene? venisti perdere nos? scio te quis sis, Sanctus Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων, ἕα τί ἡμῖν καὶ σοί ἰησοῦ ναζαρηνέ ἦλθες ἀπολέσαι ἡμᾶς οἶδά σε τίς εἶ ὁ ἅγιος τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Saying, Let *us* alone; what have we to do with thee, *thou* Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et increpavit illum Jesus, dicens: Obmutesce, et exi ab eo. Et cum projecisset illum dæmonium in medium, exiit ab illo, nihilque illum nocuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπετίμησεν αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς λέγων, φιμώθητι καὶ ἔξελθε ἐξ αὐτοῦ καὶ ῥῖψαν αὐτὸν τὸ δαιμόνιον εἰς τὸ μέσον ἐξῆλθεν ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ μηδὲν βλάψαν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him. And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factus est pavor in omnibus, et colloquebantur ad invicem, dicentes: Quod est hoc verbum, quia in potestate et virtute imperat immundis spiritibus, et exeunt?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο θάμβος ἐπὶ πάντας καὶ συνελάλουν πρὸς ἀλλήλους λέγοντες τίς ὁ λόγος οὗτος ὅτι ἐν ἐξουσίᾳ καὶ δυνάμει ἐπιτάσσει τοῖς ἀκαθάρτοις πνεύμασιν καὶ ἐξέρχονται
verses.king_james: And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word *is* this! for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et divulgabatur fama de illo in omnem locum regionis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξεπορεύετο ἦχος περὶ αὐτοῦ εἰς πάντα τόπον τῆς περιχώρου
verses.king_james: And the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgens autem Jesus de synagoga, introivit in domum Simonis. Socrus autem Simonis tenebatur magnis febribus: et rogaverunt illum pro ea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναστὰς δὲ ἐκ τῆς συναγωγῆς εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν σίμωνος ἡ πενθερὰ δὲ τοῦ σίμωνος ἦν συνεχομένη πυρετῷ μεγάλῳ καὶ ἠρώτησαν αὐτὸν περὶ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And he arose out of the synagogue, and entered into Simon's house. And Simon's wife's mother was taken with a great fever; and they besought him for her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et stans super illam imperavit febri: et dimisit illam. Et continuo surgens, ministrabat illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπιστὰς ἐπάνω αὐτῆς ἐπετίμησεν τῷ πυρετῷ καὶ ἀφῆκεν αὐτήν παραχρῆμα δὲ ἀναστᾶσα διηκόνει αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem sol occidisset, omnes qui habebant infirmos variis languoribus, ducebant illos ad eum. At ille singulis manus imponens, curabat eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δύνοντος δὲ τοῦ ἡλίου πάντες ὅσοι εἶχον ἀσθενοῦντας νόσοις ποικίλαις ἤγαγον αὐτοὺς πρὸς αὐτόν ὁ δὲ ἑνὶ ἑκάστῳ αὐτῶν τὰς χεῖρας ἐπιθεὶς, ἐθεράπευσεν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exibant autem dæmonia a multis clamantia, et dicentia: Quia tu es Filius Dei: et increpans non sinebat ea loqui: quia sciebant ipsum esse Christum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξήρχετο δὲ καὶ δαιμόνια ἀπὸ πολλῶν κράζοντα καὶ λέγοντα ὅτι σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἐπιτιμῶν οὐκ εἴα αὐτὰ λαλεῖν ὅτι ᾔδεισαν τὸν χριστὸν αὐτὸν εἶναι
verses.king_james: And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking *them* suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta autem die egressus ibat in desertum locum, et turbæ requirebant eum, et venerunt usque ad ipsum: et detinebant illum ne discederet ab eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γενομένης δὲ ἡμέρας ἐξελθὼν ἐπορεύθη εἰς ἔρημον τόπον καὶ οἱ ὄχλοι ἐζήτουν αὐτόν καὶ ἦλθον ἕως αὐτοῦ καὶ κατεῖχον αὐτὸν τοῦ μὴ πορεύεσθαι ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ille ait: Quia et aliis civitatibus oportet me evangelizare regnum Dei: quia ideo missus sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτοὺς ὅτι καὶ ταῖς ἑτέραις πόλεσιν εὐαγγελίσασθαί με δεῖ τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ ὅτι εἲς τοῦτο ἀπεστάλμαι
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat prædicans in synagogis Galilææ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν κηρύσσων ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς τῆς γαλιλαίας
verses.king_james: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.
